name: ComputeStrangeAttractor
description:
Strange Attractor — particles integrate through one of five chaotic ODEs (Lorenz / Rössler / Aizawa / Thomas / Halvorsen). Decomposed onto node.wgsl_compute (the JSON-editable shader escape hatch): the per-particle RK2 substep loop + 3D→2D projection lives in one editable WGSL string, with the attractor family selected by a `switch (attractor_type)`. Adding Sprott / Chen / Chua is a JSON edit — append a case to the switch and an entry to the per-attractor center/scale/dt const arrays. Auto-init: seed_particles(OnceOnReset) zeroes velocity; the simulate shader detects near-zero velocity per particle and runs hash-seed + warmup steps to converge onto the manifold, then proceeds with 8 RK2 substeps per frame. NaN/divergence guard teleports any particle whose 3D state escapes a large radius back to centre — a per-substep numerical safety net replacing the bundled monolith's 50-step escape respawn. Diffusion is now a separate node.array_diffuse_particles atom (reusable for any noise-jittered particle system).

Brightness compensates for canvas size via node.canvas_area_scale × 3.0 → reinhard.intensity. Camera angle = time × speed × 0.25 derives from two node.affine_scalar nodes, exposed as a small wire chain so an author can swap in an LFO or driver if they want non-linear sweep. clip_trigger uses node.clip_trigger_cycle (uniqueness invariant) + node.mux_scalar to swap between manual and trigger-driven attractor selection.
